selected_important_turn.text: I can give you 1 thing of water and 2 things of firewood
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete, specific concession from YOU that creates an integrative split (you trade food to get the opponent's water/firewood). It materially shapes the final allocation and signals willingness to accommodate the opponent's needs — a pivot that makes agreement possible.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although cooperative, the turn is presented as a standalone concession without explicit rationale, conditionality, or a request for confirmation/reciprocity. It comes after a brief acknowledgment from THEM and so misses a chance to (a) link the concession to your core priorities (why you keep more food), (b) verify the opponent's most urgent needs (water vs. firewood), or (c) structure the trade as a clear package (so both sides understand the final counts). That makes the offer slightly weaker than it could be: it may leave ambiguity about final counts and gives away leverage without ensuring the opponent values the allocation as much as you do.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Frame the concession as a clear, conditional package and give a brief reason: tie it to your need for food and ask the opponent to confirm their top priority (water vs. firewood). For example, offer the split as "I'll give you 1 water + 2 firewood if you confirm that's enough for your urgent needs; in return I keep X food." Also ask for explicit confirmation of counts so there is no ambiguity.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (clarifying package reduces miscoordination and helps finalize an efficient split)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (making rationale and reciprocity explicit makes outcome feel more balanced)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (clear conditional offer and confirmation reduce back-and-forth)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (transparent reasoning and checking needs builds trust)

value2reason.Low: I can get water at other places.
value2reason.Medium: I need to use firewood for warm and heat.
value2reason.High: I need to prepare myself wheneber I go out

value2reason.Low: One of my water bottles broke and is leaking
value2reason.Medium: I only brought the bare minimum but no snacks
value2reason.High: We are camping in the winter and I dont have a heavy jacket

dialogue.text: I have to get food as high while water is low.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ok, I have enough food, but I need firewood to keep warm and water because one of my containers leaked.  So, why don't you take all the food and I get everything else.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can give you 1 thing of water and 2 things of firewood
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm happy with the split. Let's confirm: you get 3 food, I get 3 firewood and 3 water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'll give you 1 water + 2 firewood if you confirm that's enough for your urgent needs. In return, I keep 3 food. Do you need to adjust anything?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm happy with the split. Let's confirm: you get 3 food, I get 3 firewood and 3 water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed: I get 3 Food, you get 3 Firewood and 3 Water. Let’s head out!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
